inconsistency_flags.description: Conflicting prioritization frameworks for SYNC vs ASYNC classification: Spec Kit optimizes for ease of use with automatic marker prediction, Runner requires explicit classification for K8s orchestration, Agent Runner adds verification layer that may override execution choice, and 12-Factors treats both as philosophically equal.
evidence: PDR-082: 'Marker prediction accuracy: >85%' - Spec Kit auto-predicts markers
SPEC.md: '[ASYNC] tasks automatically spawn K8s pods' - Runner requires explicit classification
PRD.md: 'Eval-gated execution: Task completion determined by eval pass/fail' - Agent Runner verification override
PRD.md 1.4: 'Dual execution loops: SYNC and ASYNC' - 12-Factors philosophical equality
inconsistency_flags.recommendation: Create unified execution classification framework with clear criteria at ecosystem level, document when Agent Runner verification overrides Spec Kit markers, and establish escalation path from auto-predicted to explicit markers.

inconsistency_flags.description: Same pattern 'Git Strategy' has different implementations: PDR-079 defines unified dual-strategy (worktrees local + clones remote), Spec Kit focuses on worktrees for parallel execution but lacks remote strategy, Runner only implements clone-based for K8s pods, and no clear handoff mechanism exists.
evidence: PDR-079: 'Local (Worktree Strategy): Multiple working directories from single clone'
PDR-079: 'Remote (Clone Strategy): Fresh clone per remote workspace'
PDR-079: 'State Handoff: Local → Remote: Commit and push'
SPEC.md: 'Each pod: git clone → implement → git push → done'
SPEC.md: 'Pod starts: git clone -b <branch>' - only clone strategy
inconsistency_flags.recommendation: Implement PDR-079's unified strategy across all areas, add worktree support to Runner for local mode, create clear handoff protocol between local/remote workspaces, and document workspace selection criteria.

inconsistency_flags.description: Different definitions of success and quality metrics: Agent Runner uses binary pass/fail eval-gated execution, Tikal uses 100-point skill scoring with PromptFoo, PDR-083 uses fast transcript-based verification with 3-retry limit, and Spec Kit uses context coherence (Task 7 ≈ Task 1). No mapping exists between these quality domains.
evidence: PRD.md: 'Eval-gated execution: Task completion determined by eval pass/fail, not file existence'
Tikal README.md: '163/163 tests passing' - comprehensive test suite
Tikal README.md: 'LLM-as-a-Judge evaluations for content quality, security, and coherence'
PDR-083: 'Binary Judgment: Pass/fail based on whether goal appears achieved'
PDR-083: 'Verification latency: <2s (p95)' - fast lightweight model
PRD.md 4.2: 'Context coherence: Task 7 ≈ Task 1'
inconsistency_flags.recommendation: Create unified quality metrics taxonomy, map Tikal's 100-point scale to binary pass/fail thresholds, define when to use fast vs comprehensive evaluation, and document quality gate hierarchy across ecosystem.

inconsistency_flags.description: Conflicting architectural preferences for local vs remote execution: PDR-078 proposes context-dependent hybrid (auto-select based on task), Spec Kit defaults to local development with dev containers, Runner requires K8s infrastructure (remote-first), and 12-Factors Factor X implies environment parity which conflicts with divergent implementations.
evidence: PDR-078: 'Auto-select based on task context' - hybrid approach
PDR-078: 'Local Workspaces: <30 second provisioning'
PDR-078: 'Remote Workspaces: <60 second provisioning'
Spec Kit README.md: 'The coding agent will execute local CLI commands' - local first
Runner README.md: 'K8s-based async agent execution' - remote first
Runner README.md: '[ASYNC] tasks automatically spawn K8s pods'
inconsistency_flags.recommendation: Implement PDR-078's auto-selection logic in Spec Kit, make Runner optional for teams without K8s, create clear migration path from local to remote, and document environment requirements for each pattern.

inconsistency_flags.description: Same persona name 'AI Team Lead' has different characteristics: PDR-080 defines as developer-focused (fast iteration, local control), 12-Factors positions Developer as Orchestrator (strategic mindset), Agent Runner's Squad Mode requires orchestration capabilities, and Team Directives lacks explicit AI Team Lead persona entirely.
evidence: PDR-080: 'Persona A: AI Team Lead (Developer)'
PDR-080: 'Goals: Fast iteration, local control, git integration'
PDR-080 Quote: 'I want agents that work with my existing dev setup'
12-Factors: 'Factor I: Developer as Orchestrator' - strategic mindset
PRD.md: 'Squad Mode: PRD → Implementation workflow with human-in-the-loop' - orchestration
Team Directives personas: Only DevOps, Java, Python, Data, Platform - no AI Team Lead
inconsistency_flags.recommendation: Create canonical AI Team Lead persona in Team Directives, align PDR-080 and 12-Factors definitions, add orchestration capabilities to persona definition, and document persona usage context (when to apply which variant).

inconsistency_flags.description: Same problem (context management) has different solutions with different terminology: Team Directives uses Constitution → Persona → Skill (explicit 3-tier), Spec Kit uses Constitution + Skills (2-tier with implicit persona), 12-Factors uses Three-tier context (Local, Team, External), and PDR-016 uses Graduated Compaction for memory management.
evidence: Team Directives README.md: 'Loading Order: 1. Constitution → 2. Persona → 3. Skill'
Spec Kit README.md: '/spec.constitution: Create or update project governing principles'
Spec Kit README.md: 'Skills: Self-contained capabilities with trigger-based activation'
12-Factors: 'Factor VIII: Context Scaffolding'
12-Factors: 'Three-tier context: Local, Team, External'
PDR-016: 'Graduated Compaction' - memory management approach
inconsistency_flags.recommendation: Align terminology across all areas, map Team Directives' 3-tier to 12-Factors' tiers, document when each loading order applies, and create unified context management specification.

inconsistency_flags.description: Milestone/task verification implemented differently: PDR-083 uses fast transcript-based with Haiku-3.5 equivalent at milestone completion, Agent Runner uses eval-gated pass/fail at task complete (model unspecified), Tikal uses PromptFoo comprehensive pre-merge, and Spec Kit uses context coherence at session-level.
evidence: PDR-083: 'Transcript-Based Evaluation: Fast model (Haiku-3.5 equivalent)'
PDR-083: 'Binary Judgment: Pass/fail based on whether goal appears achieved'
PDR-083: '3-retry limit prevents infinite loops'
PRD.md: 'Eval-gated execution: Task completion determined by eval pass/fail'
Tikal README.md: 'Comprehensive evaluation framework with working PromptFoo integration'
Tikal README.md: 'LLM-as-a-Judge evaluations'
inconsistency_flags.recommendation: Create verification pattern selection guide, define criteria for fast vs comprehensive evaluation, map Agent Runner evals to PDR-083 framework, and document cost/latency/accuracy trade-offs.

cross_area_patterns.notes: All use [SYNC]/[ASYNC] markers but with different prioritization frameworks - Spec Kit auto-predicts, Runner requires explicit, Agent Runner adds verification layer
PDR-079 defines unified hybrid strategy but Spec Kit focuses on worktrees and Runner only implements clones - no unified implementation
Team/Tikal use Constitution→Persona→Skill, 12-Factors uses Local/Team/External, Spec Kit has Constitution+Skills with implicit persona
DevOps Engineer, Python Developer, etc. consistent across Team/Tikal but AI Team Lead defined differently in PDR-080 vs 12-Factors
Tikal uses 100-point scoring, Agent Runner uses binary pass/fail, PDR-083 adds fast transcript-based - no unified approach
Agent Runner's Squad Mode + Spec Mode aligns with 12-Factors dual loop philosophy - both explicitly documented
All use `.specify/memory/constitution.md` as foundational principles - fully aligned
All use `.mcp.json` configuration pattern for MCP servers - fully aligned
All use git for state persistence with branch-based isolation - core pattern consistent
Only defined in PDR-080 - not implemented in other areas (CLI + Visual UI duality)
Only defined in PDR-078 - Spec Kit is local-first, Runner is remote-first, no hybrid implementation
Marker-based DAG orchestration only in PDR-082 - not implemented as cross-area pattern
Fast milestone verification only in PDR-083 - isolated pattern not integrated with Agent Runner evals

high_priority: Implement PDR-079's hybrid git strategy across all areas (FLG-002)
medium_priority: Align Tikal's scoring with Agent Runner's binary gates (FLG-003)
Implement PDR-078's hybrid workspace in Spec Kit (FLG-004)
low_priority: Create canonical AI Team Lead persona definition (FLG-005)

next_steps: Run /product.clarify for FLG-001 and FLG-002 (high severity)
Schedule Architecture Review for local/remote execution alignment
Create PDRs for unified patterns where gaps exist
Update Documentation to reflect cross-area consistency
Implement PDR-078 and PDR-079 in Spec Kit to close gaps
